non_claims.why_not_claimed_in_m5: M5 stayed bounded/local/artifact-backed and never proved distributed ownership.
non_claims.risk_if_accidentally_claimed: Creates false fleet/runtime expectations unsupported by retained evidence.
non_claims.wording_guardrail: Do not claim distributed worker or scheduler ownership.

non_claims.why_not_claimed_in_m5: M5 never implemented or proved deployment packaging.
non_claims.risk_if_accidentally_claimed: Users may assume deployment packaging or cluster readiness exists.
non_claims.wording_guardrail: Do not describe M5 artifacts as cloud, Kubernetes, or Helm bundles.

non_claims.why_not_claimed_in_m5: S5 was a reference bundle, not deployment packaging.
non_claims.risk_if_accidentally_claimed: Turns reference truth into a fake deployment deliverable.
non_claims.wording_guardrail: Use release-consumable reference bundle, never deployment package.

non_claims.why_not_claimed_in_m5: No installer was built or validated in M5.
non_claims.risk_if_accidentally_claimed: Would overstate onboarding and user readiness.
non_claims.wording_guardrail: Do not imply install flow or setup automation is done.

non_claims.why_not_claimed_in_m5: S6 proved only bounded same-host operational envelope.
non_claims.risk_if_accidentally_claimed: Conflates bounded envelope with real load qualification.
non_claims.wording_guardrail: Do not describe bounded same-host classes as load testing.

non_claims.why_not_claimed_in_m5: M5 froze technical truth packages, not product SLA policy.
non_claims.wording_guardrail: Do not turn operational signals into SLO/SLA claims.

non_claims.why_not_claimed_in_m5: Capability and usage truths remain technical, non-commercial.
non_claims.risk_if_accidentally_claimed: Misreads raw technical usage as monetization or entitlement.
non_claims.wording_guardrail: Base/Pro/Extra are technical capability domains, not pricing or entitlement.

non_claims.why_not_claimed_in_m5: Governance stayed audit/manual-action truth, not approval workflow.
non_claims.risk_if_accidentally_claimed: Suggests authorization controls that do not exist.
non_claims.wording_guardrail: Do not equate governance receipts with RBAC or approval workflow.

non_claims.risk_if_accidentally_claimed: Overstates connector support boundary and product maturity.
non_claims.wording_guardrail: Do not describe retained connectors as enterprise-managed.

non_claims.why_not_claimed_in_m5: M5 never introduced commercial platform or gateway surfaces.
non_claims.risk_if_accidentally_claimed: Turns technical validation into unsupported commercial platform claim.
non_claims.wording_guardrail: Do not add commercial gateway language to technical closeout artifacts.

non_claims.why_not_claimed_in_m5: M5 stayed representative and bounded by design across S1-S7.
non_claims.risk_if_accidentally_claimed: Erases the deliberate representative-only validation boundary.
non_claims.wording_guardrail: Do not describe M5 as exhaustive all-pairs equal-depth proof.

non_claims.why_not_claimed_in_m5: S8 only prepares final user-style validation and closeout.
non_claims.risk_if_accidentally_claimed: Confuses closeout prep with a finished product shell.
non_claims.wording_guardrail: Do not imply product shell UX or packaging is complete.

non_claims.why_not_claimed_in_m5: External p45/env remains authoritative runtime truth.
non_claims.risk_if_accidentally_claimed: Encourages replacing real runtime env/config with repo-only truth.
non_claims.wording_guardrail: Do not replace or shadow the authoritative external p45/env surface.
